title: quercus sessiliflora subsp. polycarpa
intro: Quercus sessiliflora subsp. polycarpa, also known as the Chinese gall oak, is a plant species that belongs to the white oak family, characterized by its unique ability to produce galls, which are abnormal growths caused by insect or fungal infections. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its high content of tannins and flavonoids. The health benefits of Quercus sessiliflora subsp. polycarpa include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat various gastrointestinal disorders, as well as its potential to protect against certain types of cancer and cardiovascular diseases. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health conditions.
